mission.title: open-webui: feat: configurable OpenAI API endpoint.
mission.description: feat: configurable OpenAI API endpoint.. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
Make the API endpoint url configurable so the user can connect other OpenAI-compatible APIs with the web-ui.

Make it configurable through environment variables or add a new field in the **Settings** > **Add-ons**.

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: configurable OpenAI API endpoint.
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: configurable OpenAI API endpoint." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: hoping to be able to contribute more, you've got the best UI going for Ollama **AND** ChatGPT/Mistral APIs right now. There's a lot of people getting interested in running local inference or using the API for privacy and solutions like this are key enablers. Here's to more in 2024! 🎉🚀

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
